sources.label: CPIC CYP2D6, OPRM1, COMT and opioid therapy
Brain CYP2D catalytic competence for serotonin formation
Brain CYP2D catalytic competence for serotonin formation
sources.note: Animal brain evidence that CYP2D can convert 5-methoxytryptamine to serotonin in vivo.

sources.label: Melatonin to serotonin through 5-methoxytryptamine and CYP2D
Melatonin to serotonin through 5-methoxytryptamine and CYP2D
sources.note: Reports a melatonin-derived route to serotonin involving 5-methoxytryptamine and CYP2D activity in brain.

sources.label: CYP2D6 converts 5-methoxytryptamine to serotonin
sources.note: Human CYP2D6 can O-demethylate 5-methoxytryptamine to serotonin in vitro, supporting an endogenous serotonin-regeneration route.

sources.label: Endocannabinoid anandamide as a CYP2D6 substrate
sources.note: Reports anandamide metabolism by polymorphic human CYP2D6, including relevance to brain/endocannabinoid metabolism.

sources.label: CYP2D6 variation, behaviour and psychopathology
sources.note: Reviews CYP2D6 brain expression, endogenous substrate hypotheses, and behavioral/psychiatric associations.

sources.label: Human CYP2D6 is functional in brain in vivo
sources.note: Evidence that CYP2D activity can be detected in human brain in vivo.

sources.label: Regional and cellular expression of CYP2D6 in human brain
sources.note: Reports CYP2D6 mRNA and protein across human brain regions.

sources.note: Review of CYP2D6 distribution, endogenous-substrate hypotheses, and CNS relevance.

sources.note: Study of CYP2D6 genetic variation effects on metabolism of endogenous neuroactive substrates.

sources.note: CYP2D6 phenotype context for codeine, tramadol, and selected opioids.

sources.label: CYP2D6 converts tyramine to dopamine
sources.note: Human CYP2D6 catalyzes tyramine hydroxylation to dopamine, supporting an alternative dopamine-formation route.

sources.label: Solanidine is a sensitive and specific dietary biomarker for CYP2D6 activity
sources.note: Reports strong CYP2D6 genotype association with plasma solanidine, including markedly higher concentrations in genetic poor metabolizers.

sources.label: Prediction of CYP2D6 poor metabolizers by solanidine and metabolites
sources.note: Metabolite-to-solanidine ratios predicted CYP2D6 poor metabolizer phenotype in genotyped patients.

sources.label: Solanidine metabolites as biomarkers of CYP2D6-mediated tamoxifen metabolism
Solanidine metabolites as diet-derived biomarkers of CYP2D6-mediated tamoxifen metabolism
sources.note: Solanidine, 4-OH-solanidine, and SSDA ratios tracked CYP2D6 activity and inhibitor effects in a tamoxifen cohort.

sources.label: SSDA as a urinary biomarker of CYP2D6 activity
sources.note: Identifies 3,4-seco-solanidine-3,4-dioic acid as a solanidine-derived CYP2D6 activity biomarker.
